title: Generation of Mutagenic Libraries using a POPCode Method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9u19e","text":"This protocol describes the use of a double-tag precision oligo-pool-based code alteration (POPCode) approach to generate a library containing nearly all possible missense variants for a gene of interest, followed by en masse transfer of the resulting amplicon pool via two steps of recombinational subcloning into destination vectors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":217,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

NEB: P0756S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a283f","text":"Taq DNA Ligase (NEB: M0208S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dtsd5","text":"QIAquick Gel Extraction Kit (28704)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7oq2s","text":"Thermolabile Exonuclease I (NEB: M0568S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a49mm","text":"Qubit dsDNA Quantification Assay Kits (Q32850, Q32851)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fa0sh","text":"Illumina MiSeq Reagent Nano Kit v2 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6uub4","text":"Gateway BP Clonase Enzyme Mix (Thermofisher Scientific: 11789013)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk5ou","text":"Gateway LR Clonase II Enzyme mix (Thermofisher Scientific: 11791020)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1nqid","text":"Corning square Bioassay Dishes (500cm2;  Millipore Sigma: CLS431111-16EA)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":37,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fj77f","text":"LB Broth (Millipore Sigma: 51208)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vaik","text":"Spectinomycin (Millipore Sigma: S0692)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fdss6","text":"Ampicillin (Millipore Sigma: A5354)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jua7","text":"NucleoBond Xtra Midi EF Kit (MACHEREY-NAGEL: 740420.50)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7qtft","text":"4% E-Gel Agarose Gels with SYBR Safe DNA Gel Stain (ThermoFisher Scientific: A42136)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sfi","text":"1 kb Plus DNA Ladder (NEB: N3200S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3q46k","text":"Agarose (Millipore Sigma: A9539)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2hrve","text":"Agar (Millipore Sigma: A6686)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d9gaf","text":"NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli (High Efficiency; C2987I, C3020K)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":22,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Protocol to generate a library of almost all possible missense variants for a gene of interest, compatible for large-scale BP into pDONR223. Visual abstract of codon mutagenesis approach :

Mutagenic Oligos For each codon along the entire length of the protein of interest, design a ~33 bp oligo with a central degenerate NNN sequence and flanking sequences that provide roughly uniform hybridization energy.

Phosphorylate Mutagenic Oligos and 3'Tag_Hyb Primer Pool 4uL of each 100μM oligo per region Phosphorylate oligos and 3'Tag_Hyb separately : %s %s * Oligos from this reaction are 6μM 3. Prepare phosphorylation reaction, flick tube, quick spin 4. Incubate 1 hour at 37C

Hybridization Split protein of interest into regions (~150AA) to control number of variants per clone. Example: Single region of a protein consists of the first 146 amino acids of the protein (meaning 146 unique mutagenic oligos). If the nucleotide length of the template is 2254 bp: n = 2 x ((number of unique oligos per region)/150) n = 2 x (146/150) =1.9. n= 2x(180)/150 = 2.4uL * Recommendation of starting with 1n and 4n. %s %s Flick and spin down tubes Denature in thermal cycler 95 o C degree for 3 min then cool to 4 o C for 15 min When 10 min passed on the first reaction, heat activate 2X master mix (MM). Put 5μL 2x Phusion MM with HF buffer into a second thermal cycler 95oC for 3 min then cool to 4oC Do not vortex after this step, just flick and spin down when you need to mix
Initial/Mutagenic Strand Extension Add 5μL of the oligo hybridization reaction to 5μL of the heat activated 2X Phusion MM Incubate 2 hours at 50oC
"Nick" Ligation %s %s Can prepare a master mix if a lot of tubes, and add 5 μL to each amplification reaction Incubate 20min at 45oC * Ligation for 1 hour can improve efficiency
Tag-Based Amplification Use 1μL of the nick ligation in a PCR reaction. * Make 3x tag based amplifications per reaction * Do not vortex, only flick and spin down %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s
Primer Cleanup Two alternative choices (9.1 and 9.2), but ExoI digestion recommended

Purifying via Gel Extraction Pool same reactions into a larger well of a 1.5% agarose gel Follow normal Qiagen gel extraction kit BR Qubit or Nanodrop to find the concentration Use 28ng/reaction for the barcode and attb addition step.

Purifying via Exonuclease I Digestion Add 5μL of exonuclease I directly to each 50μL PCR reaction Incubate at 37°C for 15 minutes Deactivate by incubating at 80°C for 15 minutes Use 1μL of the reaction for barcoding and attb addition step.

Amplification for Barcode and attb Gateway Sites addition %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s Pool the same product together, run on a 1% gel with combined wells. Gel extract product with normal Qiagen gel extraction kit. Quantify library with Qubit Ready for "Tiling" and "Indexing" for library QC * Sequencing Required : Nano Kit for Illumina MiSeq run suffices for QC of approximately 25 tiles (~30k reads/tile). Omit last tile for QC

Tiling Procedure Measuring mutations introduced requires accurate detection of very low allele frequencies, which can be challenged by the base-calling error of Illumina or other NGS platforms. However, base-calling errors can be dramatically reduced by sequencing both strands of short template amplicons (~150bp) that ' tile' the complete coding region, and only calling variants that can be detected on both strands. Forward+Reverse Oligos required for this step – custom primers for each gene for X number of tiles %s %s Thermocycler setting %s %s Analyze 4μl for each PCR reaction on homemade 2% agarose gel. Use 1μl (of 1/10 diluted) directly for indexing

Truseq Indexing Procedure * Requires i5 and i7 Truseq oligos, premixed in plates (see attached) %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s Analyze 4μl for each PCR reaction on homemade 2% agarose gel. If all samples are evenly present on gel, pool samples in equal volume to a total volume of 100μl. (eg. For 5 indexed tiles, 20μl each x 5 = 100μl) Run a 4% EX eGel, 5 lanes, 20ul in each lane for each pooled library. Gel purify and minelute to 15μl. Quantify the purified PCR pool via HS Qubit. Dilute the sample to ~5ng/μl. You need a total volume of 30μl here of the diluted samples to have enough for sequencing run.

Large Scale BP Transformation Transform BP reactions into chemically competent E. coli cells as follows. Note: The following steps are for a single region . Thaw 3 tubes of NEB-5 chemically competent E. coli cells (each contains 200 μl cells). Add 4μl BP reaction directly to each tube on ice. Prepare 3 of these mixtures per region. Incubate cells on ice for 30 min. Heat shock cells for 30 sec at 42°C in a H 2 O bath. Incubate cells on ice for 5 min. For each tube, recover 200μl cells in 3800μl of SOC Outgrowth Medium in a 50 mL falcon tube with the lid loosely taped and shake the tubes at 200 rpm at 37°C for 1 hour. Spin down the tubes at 4000 rpm for 10 min and resuspend each pellet in 500μl of LB. Plate 5μl and 10μl of culture on petri plates in duplicate for counting and the rest of the culture on large agar BioAssay dishes containing LB + 50μg/ml spectinomycin. Plate 1 reaction per BioAssay dish for a total of 3 dishes per region. Incubate the plates at 37°C overnight. Count the colonies to calculate transformation efficiency from the petri plates or directly from the large BioAssay dishes if the petri plates are not representative. Scrape the cells from the petri plates with 20 mL LB or autoclaved H 2 O and midi prep 400 or 600 ODs using the NucleoBond Xtra Midiprep Kit (MACHERY-NAGEL).

Pipeline for Library Analysis Quantify variant allele frequencies using TileSeq_MutCount: ( https://github.com/RyogaLi/tileseq_mutcount ) Count mutations/determine coverage, and further QC analysis, using tileseqMave ( https://github.com/jweile/tileseqMave )

Gateway Recombination (BP) Reaction %s %s Example: x = (amount of pDONR vector in ng) x (attB DNA nucleotide length/pDONR nucleotide length) x = (150 ng/μl x 2.5 μl) x (924 nt/5005 nt) = 69 ng Mix well by vortexing briefly and incubate at 25°C overnight (leave on the bench at room temperature)

Gateway Recombination (LR) Reaction %s %s Example: x = (BP library nucleotide length/pDEST nucleotide length) x (amount of pDEST in ng) ((2664 + 2790)/9869) x (150 ng/μl x 3 μl) = 249 ng Mix well by vortexing briefly and incubate at 25°C overnight (leave on the bench at room temperature)

Large Scale LR Transformation Note: The following steps are for a single region , multiply this protocol by the number of regions for your gene of interest. Thaw 4 tubes of NEB 5 electrocompetent E. coli cells (each contains 100μl cells) or NEB 10-beta electrocompetent cells for larger genes (>10 kB plasmid). Add 4μl LR reaction directly to each tube on ice. Prepare 4 of these mixtures per region. Electroporate 50μl cells in a 0.1 cm cuvette using the Ec1 (V = 1.8 kV) protocol on a MicroPulser Electroporator (BIO-RAD) and immediately add 1 ml of SOC or NEB 10-beta Outgrowth Medium. Pipette up and down 3 times to transfer the cells to a 50 mL falcon tube containing 1900μl of medium (total volume 4 ml). Repeat with the other 50μl of cells in the tube. Each tube requires 2 cuvettes for a total of 8 cuvettes per region. For each tube, recover 100μl cells in 3900μl of SOC or NEB 10-beta Outgrowth Medium in a 50mL falcon tube with the lid loosely taped and shake the tubes at 200 rpm at 37°C for 1 hour. Spin down the tubes at 4000 rpm for 10 min and resuspend each pellet in 500μl of LB. Plate 5μl and 10μl of culture on petri plates in duplicate for counting and the rest of the culture on large agar BioAssay dishes containing LB + 100 μg/ml ampicillin. Plate 1 reaction per BioAssay dish for a total of 4 dishes per region. Incubate the plates at 37°C overnight. Count the colonies to calculate transformation efficiency from the petri plates or directly from the large BioAssay dishes if the petri plates are not representative. Scrape the cells from the petri plates with 20 mL LB or autoclaved H 2 O and midi-prep 400 or 600 ODs using the NucleoBond Xtra Midiprep Kit (MACHERY-NAGEL). Note: You should expect >300,000 transformants
creator: Warren van Loggerenberg
